data.title: Implement scan-docs.sh text extraction (app name, stack, WCAG) and confidence context elevation
data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Context
Extends scan-docs.sh with text extraction and confidence context elevation. Builds on the file-type guard implemented in T2. The CONFIDENCE_CONTEXT contract at plugins/dso/docs/contracts/confidence-context.md specifies elevation rules: downstream stories may elevate but never lower confidence levels.

## What to Build
Extend `plugins/dso/scripts/scan-docs.sh` with:

### Extended Interface
```
scan-docs.sh <doc_folder> [--context-file=<path>]
```
- `--context-file=<path>`: optional path to existing CONFIDENCE_CONTEXT JSON file. When provided, read existing levels and apply elevation-only merge to output.

### Text Extraction Logic
For each text file that passes the file-type guard:
- Read up to 50KB of text (head the file to avoid exhausting context on large text files)
- Extract facts using keyword/pattern matching:
  - **app_name**: match patterns `App name:`, `Application:`, `Project:` (case-insensitive); extract next word/phrase on same line
  - **stack**: match `python`, `node`, `react`, `rails`, `django`, `flask`, `nextjs`, `vue`, `angular`, `spring`, `go`, `rust`, `java` (case-insensitive); extract matched term(s) as comma-separated list
  - **wcag_level**: match `WCAG (AA|AAA|A)\b` (case-insensitive); extract the level string
  - **framework**: match `framework:` prefix; extract value
- For each fact found, assign confidence:
  - Single unambiguous match → `"high"`
  - Multiple conflicting matches for same key → `"medium"`
  - No match → dimension not added to facts (absence, not low)
- Output format per fact: `{"key":"<key>","value":"<value>","confidence":"<high|medium>","source_file":"<filename>"}`

### Confidence Context Elevation
When `--context-file` is provided:
- Read existing CONFIDENCE_CONTEXT JSON
- For each extracted fact, check the corresponding dimension in existing context
- Apply element-wise max: `new_level = max(existing_level, proposed_level)` where `high > medium > low`
- Output includes `"elevated_dimensions":{<dim>:<new_level>,...}` for any dimension that changed
- Never lower a dimension — if existing is `high` and new fact suggests `medium`, keep `high`

### Updated stdout JSON schema
```json
{
  "facts": [{"key":"app_name","value":"MyApp","confidence":"high","source_file":"prd.md"}],
  "skipped": ["binary:large_file.pdf"],
  "elevated_dimensions": {"stack": "medium"}
}
```

## TDD Requirement
Tests in T3 (test-onboarding-scan-docs.sh extraction tests) must pass after this task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] test_extracts_app_name_from_text passes: temp dir text file "App name: MyApp" → facts contains app_name:MyApp
  Verify: DIR=$(mktemp -d) && echo "App name: MyApp" > $DIR/prd.md && bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/scan-docs.sh $DIR | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert any(f['key']=='app_name' and f['value']=='MyApp' for f in d['facts'])"
- [ ] test_extracts_wcag_level passes: "WCAG AA compliance" → facts contains wcag_level:AA
  Verify: DIR=$(mktemp -d) && echo "WCAG AA compliance required" > $DIR/design.md && bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/scan-docs.sh $DIR | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert any(f['key']=='wcag_level' and f['value']=='AA' for f in d['facts'])"
- [ ] test_confidence_never_lowered passes: existing high stack not lowered by partial doc signal
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-onboarding-scan-docs.sh 2>&1 | grep -q 'test_confidence_never_lowered.*PASS'
- [ ] All T3 tests pass
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-onboarding-scan-docs.sh
- [ ] scan-docs.sh exits within 5 seconds on a 1MB directory (performance: DD2)
  Verify: DIR=$(mktemp -d) && dd if=/dev/urandom bs=1M count=1 2>/dev/null > $DIR/large.pdf && time bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/scan-docs.sh $DIR 2>/dev/null; [ $? -eq 0 ]
